name: Veles
description: Veles is a major god in Slavic mythology, often associated with cattle, wealth, magic, the underworld, and trickery. He is considered a chthonic deity, ruling over the realm of the dead and the spirits of ancestors. Veles stands in opposition to the thunder god Perun, representing chaos and the earthly realm in contrast to Perun's order and the celestial sphere. The conflict between them is seen as a fundamental cosmological struggle, symbolizing the cyclical battle between order and chaos, dryness and moisture, heaven and earth. Veles is not inherently evil but rather a necessary force for balance, responsible for the natural cycles of life, death, and rebirth. He is a shape-shifter, capable of taking on various forms, often depicted as a serpent, dragon, or even a bear. He is also associated with music, poetry, and the arts, inspiring creativity and innovation. Veles is believed to protect herds of cattle and ensure prosperity, making him a popular deity among farmers and merchants. His influence extends beyond the material world, connecting to the spiritual realm and the mysteries of the afterlife. Veles's complex and multifaceted nature makes him one of the most intriguing and important figures in the Slavic pantheon.
subtitle: God of Cattle, Magic, and the Underworld

keyMyths.title: The Theft of Cattle
keyMyths.description: Veles steals Perun's cattle, leading to a cosmic battle between them. This conflict represents the struggle between chaos and order in the universe.

keyMyths.title: Veles as a Shape-shifter
keyMyths.description: Veles is known for his ability to transform into different creatures, often appearing as a serpent or a bear. This highlights his connection to the natural world and his unpredictable nature.

keyMyths.title: Guardianship of the Underworld
keyMyths.description: Veles rules over the underworld, guiding the spirits of the dead to the afterlife. He is also associated with ancestor worship and the connection between the living and the dead.
